channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: From Global to Local: Learning Context-Aware Graph Representations for Document Classification and Summarization
item.description: arXiv:2603.00021v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven method to automatically construct graph-based document representations. Building upon the recent work of Bugue\~no and de Melo (2025), we leverage the dynamic sliding-window attention module to effectively capture loc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, context, arxiv, attention, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Noise reduction in BERT NER models for clinical entity extraction
item.description: arXiv:2603.00022v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Precision is of utmost importance in the realm of clinical entity extraction from clinical notes and reports. Encoder Models fine-tuned for Named Entity Recognition (NER) are an efficient choice for this purpose, as they don't hallucinate. We pre-trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, model, arxiv, transformer, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Personalization Increases Affective Alignment but Has Role-Dependent Effects on Epistemic Independence in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.00024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are prone to sycophantic behavior, uncritically conforming to user beliefs. As models increasingly condition responses on user-specific context (personality traits, preferences, conversation history), they gain information...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, alignment, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ActMem: Bridging the Gap Between Memory Retrieval and Reasoning in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.00026v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective memory management is essential for large language model (LLM) agents handling long-term interactions. Current memory frameworks typically treat agents as passive "recorders" and retrieve information without understanding its deeper implicati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, experiment, memory, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EPPCMinerBen: A Novel Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models on Electronic Patient-Provider Communication via the Patient Portal
item.description: arXiv:2603.00028v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Effective communication in health care is critical for treatment outcomes and adherence. With patient-provider exchanges shifting to secure messaging, analyzing electronic patient-communication (EPPC) data is both essential and challenging. We introdu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, few-shot, reasoning, analysis, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Embracing Anisotropy: Turning Massive Activations into Interpretable Control Knobs for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.00029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit highly anisotropic internal representations, often characterized by massive activations, a phenomenon where a small subset of feature dimensions possesses magnitudes significantly larger than the rest. While prior ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GRIP: Geometric Refinement and Adaptive Information Potential for Data Efficiency
item.description: arXiv:2603.00031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) is increasingly governed by data efficiency rather than raw scaling volume. However, existing selection methods often decouple global distribution balancing from local instance selection, compromising th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, model, arxiv, API, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Autorubric: A Unified Framework for Rubric-Based LLM Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.00077v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rubric-based evaluation with large language models (LLMs) has become standard practice for assessing text generation at scale, yet the underlying techniques are scattered across papers with inconsistent terminology and partial solutions. We present a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> few-shot, paper, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Sourced, Multi-Agent Evidence Retrieval for Fact-Checking
item.description: arXiv:2603.00267v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Misinformation spreading over the Internet poses a significant threat to both societies and individuals, necessitating robust and scalable fact-checking that relies on retrieving accurate and trustworthy evidence. Previous methods rely on semantic and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, reasoning, context, augmented, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TraderBench: How Robust Are AI Agents in Adversarial Capital Markets?
item.description: arXiv:2603.00285v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating AI agents in finance faces two key challenges: static benchmarks require costly expert annotation yet miss the dynamic decision-making central to real-world trading, while LLM-based judges introduce uncontrolled variance on domain-specific ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, arxiv, API, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DIG to Heal: Scaling General-purpose Agent Collaboration via Explainable Dynamic Decision Paths
item.description: arXiv:2603.00309v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The increasingly popular agentic AI paradigm promises to harness the power of multiple, general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents to collaboratively complete complex tasks. While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent ro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, LLM, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Well Do Multimodal Models Reason on ECG Signals?
item.description: arXiv:2603.00312v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While multimodal large language models offer a promising solution to the "black box" nature of health AI by generating interpretable reasoning traces, verifying the validity of these traces remains a critical challenge. Existing evaluation methods are...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, alignment, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.00349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable hig...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, paper, analysis, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Confusion-Aware Rubric Optimization for LLM-based Automated Grading
item.description: arXiv:2603.00451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and unambiguous guidelines are critical for large language model (LLM) based graders, yet manually crafting these prompts is often sub-optimal as LLMs can misinterpret expert guidelines or lack necessary domain specificity. Consequently, the ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, model, arxiv, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MED-COPILOT: A Medical Assistant Powered by GraphRAG and Similar Patient Case Retrieval
item.description: arXiv:2603.00460v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical decision-making requires synthesizing heterogeneous evidence, including patient histories, clinical guidelines, and trajectories of comparable cases. While large language models (LLMs) offer strong reasoning capabilities, they remain prone to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, analysis, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transit Network Design with Two-Level Demand Uncertainties: A Machine Learning and Contextual Stochastic Optimization Framework
item.description: arXiv:2603.00010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transit Network Design is a well-studied problem in the field of transportation, typically addressed by solving optimization models under fixed demand assumptions. Considering the limitations of these assumptions, this paper proposes a new framework, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, context, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CARE: Confounder-Aware Aggregation for Reliable LLM Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.00039v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-as-a-judge ensembles are the standard paradigm for scalable evaluation, but their aggregation mechanisms suffer from a fundamental flaw: they implicitly assume that judges provide independent estimates of true quality. However, in practice, LLM ju...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, model, arxiv, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Attn-QAT: 4-Bit Attention With Quantization-Aware Training
item.description: arXiv:2603.00040v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Achieving reliable 4-bit attention is a prerequisite for end-to-end FP4 computation on emerging FP4-capable GPUs, yet attention remains the main obstacle due to FP4's tiny dynamic range and attention's heavy-tailed activations. This paper presents the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, arxiv, attention, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Econometric vs. Causal Structure-Learning for Time-Series Policy Decisions: Evidence from the UK COVID-19 Policies
item.description: arXiv:2603.00041v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal machine learning (ML) recovers graphical structures that inform us about potential cause-and-effect relationships. Most progress has focused on cross-sectional data with no explicit time order, whereas recovering causal structures from time ser...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, model, arxiv, research, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Maximizing the Spectral Energy Gain in Sub-1-Bit LLMs via Latent Geometry Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.00042v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We identify the Spectral Energy Gain in extreme model compression, where low-rank binary approximations outperform tiny-rank floating-point baselines for heavy-tailed spectra. However, prior attempts fail to realize this potential, trailing state-of-t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, vector, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reinforcement Learning for Control with Probabilistic Stability Guarantee: A Finite-Sample Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.00043v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to reinforcement learning (RL) for control systems that provides probabilistic stability guarantees using finite data. Leveraging Lyapunov's method, we propose a probabilistic stability theorem that ensures mean sq...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, paper, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Property-Driven Evaluation of GNN Expressiveness at Scale: Datasets, Framework, and Study
item.description: arXiv:2603.00044v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Advancing trustworthy AI requires principled software engineering approaches to model evaluation. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable success in processing graph-structured data, however, their expressiveness in capturing fundamental...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, model, arxiv, framework, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: REMIND: Rethinking Medical High-Modality Learning under Missingness--A Long-Tailed Distribution Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.00046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical multi-modal learning is critical for integrating information from a large set of diverse modalities. However, when leveraging a high number of modalities in real clinical applications, it is often impractical to obtain full-modality observatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, analysis, arxiv, multimodal, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, context window, context, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, chain-of-thought, CoT, framework, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, knowledge base, model, vector, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, vector, framework, LLM, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: SimpleTool: Parallel Decoding for Real-Time LLM Function Calling
item.description: arXiv:2603.00030v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based function calling enables intelligent agents to interact with external tools and environments, yet autoregressive decoding imposes a fundamental latency bottleneck that limits real-time applications such as embodied intelligence, game AI, and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, arxiv, LLM, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: TAB-PO: Preference Optimization with a Token-Level Adaptive Barrier for Token-Critical Structured Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.00025v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Direct Preference Optimization is an offline post-SFT method for aligning language models from preference pairs, with strong results in instruction following and summarization. However, DPO's sequence-level implicit reward can be brittle for token-cri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, instruction, arxiv, summarization | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Monotropic Artificial Intelligence: Toward a Cognitive Taxonomy of Domain-Specialized Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.00350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in artificial intelligence research equates progress with scale: larger models trained on broader datasets are presumed to yield superior capabilities. This assumption, while empirically productive for general-purpose applicati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, arxiv, framework, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NeuroHex: Highly-Efficient Hex Coordinate System for Creating World Models to Enable Adaptive AI
item.description: arXiv:2603.00376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: \textit{NeuroHex} is a hexagonal coordinate system designed to support highly efficient world models and reference frames for online adaptive AI systems. Inspired by the hexadirectional firing structure of grid cells in the human brain, NeuroHex adopt...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, arxiv, framework, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Breaking the Factorization Barrier in Diffusion Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.00045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion language models theoretically allow for efficient parallel generation but are practically hindered by the "factorization barrier": the assumption that simultaneously predicted tokens are independent. This limitation forces a trade-off: model...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, arxiv, framework, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, step-by-step, prompt engineering, tool, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Conservative Equilibrium Discovery in Offline Game-Theoretic Multiagent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.00374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline learning of strategies takes data efficiency to its extreme by restricting algorithms to a fixed dataset of state-action trajectories. We consider the problem in a mixed-motive multiagent setting, where the goal is to solve a game under the of...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
